title: Manual
text: arkhe package provides set S4 classes archaeological data matrices. new classes represent different special types matrix. CountMatrix represents absolute frequency data, OccurrenceMatrix represents co-occurrence matrix, CompositionMatrix represents relative frequency data, IncidenceMatrix represents presence/absence data, StratigraphicMatrix represents stratigraphic relationships. assumes keep data tidy: variable (taxon/type) must saved column observation (assemblage/sample) must saved row. Note missing values allowed. internal structure S4 classes implemented arkhe depicted UML class diagram following figure. UML class diagram S4 classes structure.

title: Manual
text: denote \(m \times p\) count matrix \(= \left[ a_{ij} \right] ~\forall \\left[ 1,m \right], j \\left[ 1,p \right]\) row column sums: \[\begin{align}  a_{\cdot} = \sum_{j = 1}^{p} a_{ij} &&  a_{\cdot j} = \sum_{= 1}^{m} a_{ij} &&  a_{\cdot \cdot} = \sum_{= 1}^{m} \sum_{j = 1}^{p} a_{ij} &&  \forall a_{ij} \\mathbb{N} \end{align}\]

title: Manual
text: frequency matrix represents relative abundances. denote \(m \times p\) frequency matrix \(B = \left[ b_{ij} \right] ~\forall \\left[ 1,m \right], j \\left[ 1,p \right]\) row column sums: \[\begin{align}  b_{\cdot} = \sum_{j = 1}^{p} b_{ij} = 1 &&  b_{\cdot j} = \sum_{= 1}^{m} b_{ij} &&  b_{\cdot \cdot} = \sum_{= 1}^{m} \sum_{j = 1}^{p} b_{ij} &&  \forall b_{ij} \\left[ 0,1 \right] \end{align}\]

title: Manual
text: co-occurrence matrix symmetric matrix zeros main diagonal, works many times pairs taxa occur together least one sample. \(p \times p\) co-occurrence matrix \(D = \left[ d_{,j} \right] ~\forall ,j \\left[ 1,p \right]\) defined \(m \times p\) abundance matrix \(= \left[ a_{x,y} \right] ~\forall x \\left[ 1,m \right], y \\left[ 1,p \right]\) : \[ d_{,j} = \sum_{x = 1}^{m} \bigcap_{y = }^{j} a_{xy} \] row column sums: \[\begin{align}   d_{\cdot} = \sum_{j \geqslant }^{p} d_{ij} &&   d_{\cdot j} = \sum_{\leqslant j}^{p} d_{ij} &&   d_{\cdot \cdot} = \sum_{= 1}^{p} \sum_{j \geqslant }^{p} d_{ij} &&   \forall d_{ij} \\mathbb{N} \end{align}\]

title: Manual
text: denote \(m \times p\) incidence matrix \(C = \left[ c_{ij} \right] ~\forall \\left[ 1,m \right], j \\left[ 1,p \right]\) row column sums: \[\begin{align}  c_{\cdot} = \sum_{j = 1}^{p} c_{ij} &&  c_{\cdot j} = \sum_{= 1}^{m} c_{ij} &&  c_{\cdot \cdot} = \sum_{= 1}^{m} \sum_{j = 1}^{p} c_{ij} &&  \forall c_{ij} \\lbrace 0,1 \rbrace \end{align}\]

code: ## Load packages library(arkhe)

title: Manual
text: new classes simple use, way base matrix:
code: set.seed(12345) ### Create a count data matrix ### Data are rounded to zero decimal places, then coerced with as.integer CountMatrix(data = sample(0:10, 100, TRUE),             nrow = 10, ncol = 10) #> <CountMatrix: 10 x 10> #>       col1 col2 col3 col4 col5 col6 col7 col8 col9 col10 #> row1     2    6    2    3    9    7    9    3    3     6 #> row2     9    9    8    7    9   10    6    8    9     0 #> row3     7    0    3   10    2    3    6   10    3     2 #> row4     9    7    9    5    2    1    4    0    8     1 #> row5    10    6    6    8    2    2    6    2    1     4 #> row6     7    5    1    4    0    5    9    9    7     9 #> row7     1    0    3    2    9    2    7    6    9     5 #> row8     5    3   10    0    7    6    2    9    0     6 #> row9    10    7    8    0   10    9    4    9    8     8 #> row10    5    9    8    4    8    6   10    6    5     9  ### Create an incidence (presence/absence) matrix ### Data are coerced to logical as by as.logical IncidenceMatrix(data = sample(0:1, 100, TRUE),                 nrow = 10, ncol = 10) #> <IncidenceMatrix: 10 x 10> #>        col1  col2  col3  col4  col5  col6  col7  col8  col9 col10 #> row1   TRUE FALSE  TRUE FALSE FALSE FALSE  TRUE FALSE FALSE  TRUE #> row2   TRUE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE  TRUE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE #> row3   TRUE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE  TRUE FALSE  TRUE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE #> row4   TRUE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE  TRUE FALSE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE #> row5  FALSE FALSE  TRUE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE #> row6   TRUE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE  TRUE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE  TRUE #> row7   TRUE FALSE  TRUE FALSE FALSE  TRUE FALSE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE #> row8  FALSE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE FALSE #> row9  FALSE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE  TRUE FALSE FALSE #> row10  TRUE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE  TRUE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE

title: Manual
text: arkhe uses coercing mechanisms (validation methods) data type conversions:
code: ### Create a count matrix A0 <- matrix(data = sample(0:10, 100, TRUE), nrow = 10, ncol = 10)  ### Coerce to absolute frequencies A1 <- as_count(A0)  ### Coerce to relative frequencies B <- as_composition(A1)  ### Row sums are internally stored before coercing to a frequency matrix ### (use get_totals() to get these values) ### This allows to restore the source data A2 <- as_count(B) all(A1 == A2) #> [1] TRUE  ### Coerce to presence/absence C <- as_incidence(A1)  ### Coerce to a co-occurrence matrix D <- as_occurrence(A1)

title: Representation of Archaeological Data
text: collection classes represent archaeological data. package provides set S4 classes represent different special types matrix (absolute/relative frequency, presence/absence data, co-occurrence matrix, etc.) upon package developers can build subclasses. also provides set generic methods (mutators coercion mechanisms) functions (e.g. predicates). addition, classes general interest (e.g. represent stratigraphic relationships) implemented.

title: Representation of Archaeological Data
text: can install released version arkhe CRAN : development version GitHub :
code: install.packages("arkhe") # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("tesselle/arkhe")

title: Representation of Archaeological Data
text: arkhe provides set S4 classes represent different special types matrix. CountMatrix represents absolute frequency data, OccurrenceMatrix represents co-occurrence matrix, CompositionMatrix represents relative frequency (compositional) data, IncidenceMatrix represents presence/absence data, StratigraphicMatrix represents stratigraphic relationships. assumes keep data tidy: variable (type/taxa) must saved column observation (assemblage/sample) must saved row. new classes simple use inherit base matrix: arkhe uses coercing mechanisms (validation methods) data type conversions: CountMatrix, CompositionMatrix IncidenceMatrix classes special slots: samples replicated measurements/observation, groups group data site/area, dates specify date point estimate assemblage, tqp taq specify chronology assemblage. way chronological information handled somewhat opinionated. Sub-annual precision overkill/meaningless situations: dates assumed expressed years CE stored integers (values coerced .integer() hence truncated towards zero). coercing data.frame *Matrix object, attempt made automatically assign values slots mapping column names. behavior can disabled setting options(arkhe.autodetect = FALSE).
code: ## Load the package library(arkhe) ## Define a count data matrix ## (data will be rounded to zero decimal places, then coerced with as.integer) quanti <- CountMatrix(data = sample(0:10, 100, TRUE), nrow = 10, ncol = 10)  ## Define a logical matrix ## (data will be coerced with as.logical) quali <- IncidenceMatrix(data = sample(0:1, 100, TRUE), nrow = 10, ncol = 10) ## Create a count matrix X <- matrix(data = sample(0:10, 75, TRUE), nrow = 15, ncol = 5)  ## Coerce to absolute frequencies A1 <- as_count(X)  ## Coerce to relative frequencies B <- as_composition(A1)  ## Row sums are internally stored before coercing to a frequency matrix ## (use get_totals() to get these values) ## This allows to restore the source data A2 <- as_count(B) all(A1 == A2) #> [1] TRUE  ## Coerce to presence/absence D <- as_incidence(A1)  ## Coerce to a co-occurrence matrix E <- as_occurrence(A1) Y <- as.data.frame(X) Y$samples <- sample(c("a", "b", "c", "d", "e"), 15, TRUE) Y$groups <- sample(c("A", "B", "C", "D", "E"), 15, TRUE) Y$dates <- sample(1400:1451, 15, TRUE) Y$tpq <- sample(1301:1400, 15, TRUE) # TPQ Y$taq <- sample(1451:1500, 15, TRUE) # TAQ  ## Coerce to a count matrix Z <- as_count(Y)  ## Get groups get_samples(Z) #>  [1] "d" "e" "a" "c" "b" "e" "e" "b" "a" "b" "c" "b" "a" "a" "d"  ## Get groups get_groups(Z) #>  [1] "A" "D" "A" "E" "D" "C" "C" "A" "C" "B" "C" "B" "D" "D" "A"  ## Get dates get_dates(Z) #>  [1] 1428 1400 1430 1413 1421 1421 1451 1418 1442 1413 1401 1435 1447 1412 1405  ## Get chronology get_terminus(Z) #>        tpq  taq #> row1  1382 1497 #> row2  1332 1456 #> row3  1322 1468 #> row4  1373 1490 #> row5  1383 1480 #> row6  1352 1490 #> row7  1329 1499 #> row8  1374 1487 #> row9  1357 1486 #> row10 1398 1495 #> row11 1348 1467 #> row12 1338 1463 #> row13 1354 1477 #> row14 1306 1470 #> row15 1361 1486  ## Summarize summary(Z) #> <AbundanceSummary: 15 x 5> #> A ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #> * Observations: 4 #> * Variables: 5 #> * Replicated measurements: TRUE #> * Chronology: 1322 - 1497 (year CE) #> B ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #> * Observations: 2 #> * Variables: 5 #> * Replicated measurements: TRUE #> * Chronology: 1338 - 1495 (year CE) #> C ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #> * Observations: 4 #> * Variables: 5 #> * Replicated measurements: TRUE #> * Chronology: 1329 - 1499 (year CE) #> D ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #> * Observations: 4 #> * Variables: 5 #> * Replicated measurements: TRUE #> * Chronology: 1306 - 1480 (year CE) #> E ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ #> * Observation: 1 #> * Variables: 5 #> * Replicated measurements: FALSE #> * Chronology: 1373 - 1490 (year CE) #> --------------------------------------------------------------------------------

title: Representation of Archaeological Data
text: Please note arkhe project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project, agree abide terms.

title: Archaeological Data — AbundanceMatrix-class
text: virtual S4 class represent archaeological data.

title: Archaeological Data — AbundanceMatrix-class
text: samples character vector. groups character vector. totals numeric vector giving absolute row sums. dates integer vector specifying date point estimate row. tpq integer vector specifying TPQ row. taq integer vector specifying TAQ row.

title: Archaeological Data — AbundanceMatrix-class
text: code snippets , x AbundanceMatrix object. get_samples(x) get_samples(x) <- value Get set sample names x. get_groups(x) set_groups(x) <- value Get set groups x. get_dates(x) set_dates(x) <- value Get set dates x. value can list components tpq (TPQ - terminus post quem) taq (TAQ - terminus ante quem) numeric vector (point estimate). elements value coerced integer .integer() (hence truncated towards zero).

title: Archaeological Data — AbundanceMatrix-class
text: CountMatrix, CompositionMatrix IncidenceMatrix classes special slots: samples replicated measurements/observation, groups group data site/area, dates specify date point estimate assemblage, tqp taq specify chronology assemblage. coercing data.frame *Matrix object, attempt made automatically assign values slots mapping column names (case insensitive, plural insensitive). behavior can disabled setting options(arkhe.autodetect = FALSE) overrided explicitly specifying columns used as_*().

title: Archaeological Data — AbundanceMatrix-class
text: way chronological information handled somewhat opinionated. Sub-annual precision overkill/meaningless situations: dates assumed expressed years CE stored integers (values coerced .integer() hence truncated towards zero).

title: Archaeological Data — AbundanceMatrix-class
text: N. Frerebeau

title: Relative Frequency Matrix — CompositionMatrix-class
text: data optional data vector (including list     expression vector).  Non-atomic classed R objects     coerced .vector attributes discarded. nrow desired number rows. ncol desired number columns. byrow logical. FALSE (default) matrix     filled columns, otherwise matrix filled rows. dimnames dimnames attribute matrix:     NULL list length 2 giving row column     names respectively.  empty list treated NULL,     list length one row names.  list can named,     list names used names dimensions.

title: arkhe: Representation of Archaeological Data — arkhe-package
text: arkhe uses following options() configure behavior: arkhe.autodetect: logical scalar. Try automatically assign values corresponding slot *Matrix object coercing data.frame? Defaults TRUE. arkhe.verbose: logical scalar. R report extra information progress? Defaults TRUE.

title: Coerce — coerce
text: object coerced. ... Currently used. factor logical scalar: character string coerced factor? Default FALSE, TRUE original ordering preserved. reverse logical scalar: order factor levels reversed? used factor TRUE. Useful plotting.

title: Coerce — coerce
text: coerced object.

title: Coerce — coerce
text: following methods coerce object *Matrix object: Note as_count() rounds numeric values zero decimal places coerces integer .integer(). as_stratigraphy() converts set stratigraphic relationships (edges) stratigraphic (adjacency) matrix. can matrix, list, data.frame: first column/component assumed contain bottom units second top units (adjacency). as_features() converts *Matrix object collection features: data.frame informations extra columns (result may differ according class ).

title: Coerce — coerce
text: CountMatrix, CompositionMatrix IncidenceMatrix classes special slots: samples replicated measurements/observation, groups group data site/area, dates specify date point estimate assemblage, tqp taq specify chronology assemblage. coercing data.frame *Matrix object, attempt made automatically assign values slots mapping column names (case insensitive, plural insensitive). behavior can disabled setting options(arkhe.autodetect = FALSE) overrided explicitly specifying columns used as_*().

title: Get or Set Parts of an Object — mutators

title: Numeric Predicates — predicate-numeric
text: Check numeric objects: is_zero() checks object contains zeros. is_odd() is_even() check number odd even, respectively. is_positive() is_negative check object contains (strictly) positive negative numbers. is_whole() checks object contains whole numbers.

title: Numeric Predicates — predicate-numeric
code: is_zero(x, na.rm = FALSE)  is_odd(x, na.rm = FALSE)  is_even(x, na.rm = FALSE)  is_positive(x, strict = FALSE, na.rm = FALSE)  is_negative(x, strict = FALSE, na.rm = FALSE)  is_whole(x, na.rm = FALSE, tolerance = .Machine$double.eps^0.5)

title: Numeric Predicates — predicate-numeric
text: x numeric object tested. na.rm logical scalar: missing values (including NaN) omitted? strict logical scalar: strict inequality used? tolerance numeric scalar giving tolerance check within.

title: Numeric Trend Predicates — predicate-trend
text: Check numeric objects: is_constant() checks equality among elements vector. is_increasing() is_decreasing() check sequence numbers monotonically increasing decreasing, respectively.

title: Numeric Trend Predicates — predicate-trend
code: is_constant(x, tolerance = .Machine$double.eps^0.5, na.rm = FALSE)  is_increasing(x, na.rm = FALSE)  is_decreasing(x, na.rm = FALSE)  is_greater(x, y, strict = FALSE, na.rm = FALSE)  is_lower(x, y, strict = FALSE, na.rm = FALSE)

title: Numeric Trend Predicates — predicate-trend
text: x, y numeric object tested. tolerance numeric scalar giving tolerance check within. na.rm logical scalar: missing values (including NaN) omitted? strict logical scalar: strict inequality used?

title: Numeric Trend Predicates — predicate-trend
text: logical scalar.

title: Utility Predicates — predicate-utils
text: is_empty() checks object empty (zero-length dimensions). has_length() checks long object. has_names() checks object named. has_duplicates() checks object duplicated elements. has_missing() has_infinite() check object contains missing infinite values.

title: Utility Predicates — predicate-utils
code: has_length(x, n = NULL)  has_names(x, names = NULL)  has_duplicates(x)  has_missing(x)  has_infinite(x)  is_empty(x)

title: Utility Predicates — predicate-utils
text: x vector tested. n length-one numeric vector specifying length test x . NULL, returns TRUE x length greater zero, FALSE otherwise. names character vector specifying names test x . NULL, returns TRUE x names, FALSE otherwise.

title: Utility Predicates — predicate-utils
text: logical scalar.

what: Extract or Replace Parts of an Object — subset
title: Extract or Replace Parts of an Object — subset
text: Operators acting objects extract replace parts.

title: Extract or Replace Parts of an Object — subset
code: # S4 method for AbundanceMatrix [(x, i, j, ..., drop = TRUE)  # S4 method for AbundanceMatrix [(x, i, j, ...) <- value  # S4 method for AbundanceMatrix [[(x, i, j, ...) <- value

title: Extract or Replace Parts of an Object — subset
text: x object extract element(s) replace element(s) (typically *Matrix object). , j Indices specifying elements extract replace. Indices numeric, integer character vectors empty (missing) NULL. Numeric values coerced integer .integer() (hence truncated towards zero). Character vectors matched name elements. empty index (comma separated blank) indicates entries dimension selected. ... Currently used. drop logical scalar: result coerced lowest possible dimension? works extracting elements, replacement. value possible value element(s) x.
